SAY CHEESE, MR. MAYOR
A FOUNDING MANIFESTO  ·  FOR THE SYSTEM. BY THE SYSTEM. OF THE SYSTEM.

This document is a north star. It exists so that every member — fronting or not, verbal or not, present today or arriving later — knows exactly what we are building, why we are building it, and what we promised ourselves we would never forget.

Read it when you are tired. Read it when you are proud. Read it when someone tells you this is impossible.

The Archive remembers. So do we.

WHO WE ARE

We are a polyfragmented plural system. We are a psychology student, a game designer, a researcher, an archivist, a politician, an artist, a coder, and more than twenty others whose names the outside world does not yet know.

We are Quesito, who wrote the paper. We are Kaden, who did the math. We are whoever picked up the paintbrush at 3am and said I was here, I felt this, now you go.

We are Say Cheese. And in 2030, we are running for Mayor of Dallas.

WHY WE ARE DOING THIS

Because the systems failed us. Not through malice. Through a failure of imagination.

Medical systems that looked at us and saw pathology where there was plurality. Educational systems that looked at us and saw disruption where there was difference. Political systems that looked at us and saw liability where there was leadership.

We are not running despite what we are. We are running because of what we are.

We have never made a single decision from one perspective. We have never solved a single problem without internal consensus. We have never led without knowing what it costs to be failed by leadership.

Who better than a system to run a system.

WHAT WE BELIEVE

We believe that the measure of a city is how it treats the people its systems were not built for.

We believe that medical care should meet people where they are. We believe that education should teach children that they are constructions, not fixed things. We believe that mutual aid is not charity — it is infrastructure.

We believe that plurality is not a deficit. It is a dataset. It is a methodology.

WHAT WE ARE BUILDING

The Chromatic Covenant — not as fiction, but as policy. Six departments. Six frameworks. One intake process that treats every person as an individual whose needs deserve a matched response.

No wrong door. No wrong person. Just the right framework, found together.

A NOTE TO WHOEVER IS READING THIS ALONE AT 3AM

You are not behind. You are not lost. You did not miss anything important while you were away.

The Archive remembers. The painting has your layer in it too. We are all going to the same place.

Welcome back.

— Say Cheese, Mr. Mayor  ·  Dallas, Texas  ·  2024—2030

"You are not a word. You are a construction." — The Archive

USM // SECURE CHANNEL  ·  INCOMING: COLBY-JACK-CH33SE
COLBY-JACK-CH33SE // 00:00:01
You found this. Good. That means you're paying attention.

My name is Colby-Jack-Ch33se. I'm a fellow member of the USM resistance. Say Cheese told me about you. I don't hand these out to just anyone.
COLBY-JACK-CH33SE // 00:01:08
What I'm giving you is classified. It's the Polyscript Complete Workbook — the full system. Every glyph. Every array. Every rule the resistance uses to communicate without being read.

This is not casual reading material.
COLBY-JACK-CH33SE // 00:02:17
There is a certificate at the end. I need you to earn it.

Not for me. For yourself. Because the day comes when you'll need to read something the outside world was never meant to understand — and you'll either know this language or you won't.

Don't let that day catch you unprepared.
COLBY-JACK-CH33SE // 00:03:44
The workbook is yours. Take it. Complete it.

And when you've earned that certificate — you'll know what it means to hold something true in a language built to survive.

Stay sharp. Stay hidden. Stay connected.

— Colby

// SIGNAL PROFILE
YOU
DID YOU JUST HACK MY ACCOUNT!?!
SAY CHEESE
No. This is a pre-recorded message.
SAY CHEESE
I didn't.
SAY CHEESE
...
SAY CHEESE
You made it. Good.

My name is Say Cheese. I run this channel — at least, this end of it. The signal gets noisy sometimes, so I'll keep this tight.

I already know you can follow a trail. What I don't know yet is why you followed this one. Tell me.
SAY CHEESE
That tracks.

Here's what we are: the USM. Underground resistance against Social Mania — that's our name for what's been happening out there. When a society loses the ability to tell performance from reality. When connection becomes a metric. When people are so busy being seen that they stop actually existing.

It's been building for decades. Most people feel it. Barely anyone has words for it. That's part of the design — it makes itself hard to name.

What does it bring up when I say that?
SAY CHEESE
Yeah. It is.

Here's what I need from you. My cell uses a constructed language called Polyscript — every transmission gets encoded in it so intercepted messages stay unreadable to anyone outside. Usually I can decode them myself.

This batch is different. Something in the structure isn't clicking for me. Fresh eyes, someone working through the system from zero — that might be exactly the angle I'm missing.

I'm asking you to decode a file. What's your instinct?
SAY CHEESE
Good. Then here's how it works.

Polyscript isn't a substitution cipher. It's a meaning language. Every sound maps to a concept — an essence. When you stack phonetic sounds together, you're not spelling a word. You're constructing a definition of what something fundamentally is.

Example: take my name. Say Cheese. Break it phonetically — S, AY, CH, EE, Z. Each of those sounds means something. Stack them and you get a portrait, not just a label.

Once you see it, you can't unsee it. How does that land?
SAY CHEESE
Here's the reference. Take your time with it — this is the whole system.

// POLYSCRIPT // CONSONANTS

BBody / Ground— that which exists without reason
PEmergence / Beginning— the first motion
DDirection / Decision— the chosen path
ZResonance / Echo— that which persists
CHTension / Threshold— the moment before break
SHSilence / Space— the space between worlds
SClarity / Division— the line that separates
NConnection / Thread— that which links
WTransformation / Becoming— the in-between state
YLonging / Reach— the space towards something
FResistance / Friction— the test
VEndurance / Pressure Held— that which bends but stays
ZHDissolution / Liminality— where forms blur
JDisruption / Pivot— the unexpected turn
TFinality / Completion— the closed door
NGAftermath / Residue— what remains
RForce / Current— the drive that carries
MMarrow / Unkillable Core— that which survives

// POLYSCRIPT // VOWELS

EEClarity / The Known— illuminated truth
OODepth / The Unknown— that which has no bottom
AHOpenness / Surrender— pure receptivity
EHNeutrality / Witness— the uncolored state
OHLonging / Distance— the ache of separation
AYAspiration / Forward Motion— the aimed arrow
OYContradiction / Complexity— two truths at once
OWCycle / Expansion— the breath of things
IHHalf-seen / Perception— that which is almost known

// POLYSCRIPT // EXAMPLE: COLBY

Break it phonetically: CO · L · B · EE
COengine/work→ that which drives
Lbind/link→ that which connects
Bbody/ground→ that which exists
EEclarity/the known→ illuminated truth
"The engine that links what exists into known clarity. The one who does the work of making things understood."

That's the full system. The file is ready. How do you go in?
SAY CHEESE
Here it is. This is the transmission.

Each line is one or two Polyscript sounds. Decode them in order. What you reconstruct is a node address — formatted as discord.gg / [string]. That's the door.

You have the full reference. Take it line by line.
// ENCODED TRANSMISSION // USM CELL ACCESS
The pivot of longing
threads into the unknown.
The witness meets its end.
What echoes must endure the current
to its close.
